doc: Per-method classification used by the website to render Detailed Results chips. Each method carries (a) a heaviness level (low/medium/high/extreme) determined by typical peak working set and CPU time per call, and (b) an optional lifecycle status (active/deprecated/retired) with the version it changed in. Match is case-insensitive substring on the bench label; longest match wins.

low.label: Low
low.explanation: Cheap and bounded. Safe to run on any host.

medium.label: Medium
medium.explanation: Modest CPU + RAM. Fine on laptops; expect a few seconds.

high.label: High
high.explanation: Heavy CPU and/or hundreds of MiB peak RSS. Use a dedicated build box if your laptop is small.

extreme.label: Extreme
extreme.explanation: Multi-GiB peak RSS or multi-second CPU per call. Do not run on machines under 32 GiB RAM.

deprecated.label: Deprecated
deprecated.explanation: Public API stays callable so existing blobs / call sites keep working, but the method is no longer recommended for new code. Emits a deprecation warning at compile time and (where applicable) at first call. See CHANGELOG for the alternative.

retired.label: Retired
retired.explanation: Same as deprecated, plus a one-time runtime banner with the retirement story. Existing blobs still decode; nothing new is built on top. Will be removed in a future major bump.

methods.notes: Deprecated in 3.7.0 — non-standard chained hash (SHA-256 → SHA-1 → SHA-512 → SHA-256). Includes a SHA-1 hop with known collision weaknesses; output is 256 bits despite the '513' name. Use hash512 (SHA-512) or SHA3-512 for new code. Existing call sites continue to work.
stdlib base64; O(N) but bounded buffers.

Numeric encoding: turns arbitrary bytes into a fixed-10-digit-block string with an embedded FNV-1a checksum. Not obfuscation — the point is producing digit-only output for channels that only carry digits (SMS, phone fields, certain DBs). Sibling-in-spirit of kFMe (turn-data-into-numbers). Stays active as long as it stays fast.
Deprecated in 3.7.0 — SHA-256 with a custom prefilter (no added security). Use hash512 / uhash513 instead. Existing blobs still encode/decode.

🫡 Retired in 3.7.0. First encoding method in BaseFWX (V1, proof-of-concept era). Non-streaming whole-buffer NumPy path; peak RSS ≈ 5–6× input. Use base64 / hash512 for new code. Existing blobs still decode. ❤️
Deprecated in 3.7.0 — reversible obfuscation codec with no security goal; slower than base64 for the same output. Calls b256 internally. Existing blobs still decode.
KDF + streaming AEAD + ffmpeg pipe; multi-MiB working set.
Argon2id + AES-256-GCM; KDF dominates wall time.
File mode encrypt with master key; full Argon2 + ML-KEM wrap + AEAD over the whole file.
Inverse of kFMe; same memory profile.
File mode encrypt with archive packing; multi-GiB capable.
AN7 stealth anonymisation; streaming with small working set.
